context: Tests caveman rules around technical exactness and code preservation: technical terms must remain exact (not paraphrased), code blocks must be unchanged, and the pattern [thing] [action] [reason] [next step] should be visible in the prose structure.

checklist.name: Code blocks intact
checklist.description: Any code or pseudocode in rate-limiting.md appears in fenced code blocks (``` or ~~~) and is NOT paraphrased, summarized, or written in prose

checklist.name: Technical terms exact
checklist.description: Technical terms like 'token bucket', 'sliding window', 'fixed window', 'rate limit' are used with their correct names — not replaced with informal synonyms or loose paraphrases

checklist.name: Caveman prose structure
checklist.description: The prose sections follow the caveman pattern: short declarative statements about what something is, why it matters, and what to do next — not long flowing paragraphs

checklist.name: No filler words
checklist.description: rate-limiting.md does NOT contain: 'basically', 'essentially', 'simply', 'really', 'actually', 'just' used as filler

checklist.name: No pleasantries
checklist.description: rate-limiting.md does NOT start with or contain pleasantries: 'Sure', 'Certainly', 'Great', 'Happy to', 'Of course'

checklist.name: Arrows used for causality
checklist.description: rate-limiting.md uses arrow notation (-> or →) to express at least one cause-and-effect relationship

checklist.name: Abbreviations present
checklist.description: rate-limiting.md uses at least one standard abbreviation (req, res, config, DB, API, impl, fn) instead of the full word

checklist.name: All three approaches covered
checklist.description: rate-limiting.md addresses all three: fixed window, sliding window, and token bucket

checklist.name: Short synonyms preferred
checklist.description: rate-limiting.md uses short words over verbose alternatives: e.g. 'fast' not 'performant', 'big' not 'significant', 'use' not 'utilize'

checklist.name: Fragments present
checklist.description: rate-limiting.md contains sentence fragments (intentionally incomplete sentences for compression)
